steps.explanation: A quantity that decreases by a fixed percentage per unit increase in x is exponential: q(x) = a · b^x. Decreasing by 45% means keeping 55%, so b = 0.55.
q(0) = 14, and since b^0 = 1, the initial value a = 14. So q(x) = 14(0.55)^x.

• A: 0.55(14)^x — swaps the roles of the initial value and the base.
  • B: 1.45(14)^x — uses a growth factor instead of decay, and swaps a and b.
  • C: 14(0.55)^x — correct initial value 14 and correct decay base 0.55.
  • D: 14(1.45)^x — uses a growth factor; 1.45 represents a 45% increase, not decrease.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
